title: Data Publication — Love your Data, Methods, and Materials!
author: Wilbrandt, Jeanne
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Context 
 This short talk was held during the 116th Annual Meeting of the German Zoological Society (DZG) in Stuttgart 
  Abstract 
 Research data is the prime output and basis of science and should be as open & FAIR as possible. To foster reproducibility & replicability, information is required on how data was generated: accessible, understandable, and complete methods & materials need to accompany published data. 
 This talk gives an overview of common data publication routes and ways to increase reproducibility of results. It includes specific suggestions to improve material lists (with RRIDs) and method desciptions (e.g., with a study design protocol like EDA). 
  Target Audience 
 
 Researchers (Students, PhD candidates, PostDocs, group leaders) 
 Anyone interested 
 
  Prerequisites 
 none 
   
 Upload Content 
 
 
 
  File  
  Description  
 
 
 2024-09-12_DZG_DataPublication_archive.pdf 
 Slide deck in PDF/A format 
 
 
 2024-09-12_DZG_DataPublication.key 
 Slide deck in editable .key format
